references.notes: The term "المقادر" (fates/destinies) refers to the pre-Islamic cosmological concept of fate as an irresistible, often destructive, force.
The image of a fluttering bird in the context of death and grief alludes to the pre-Islamic belief in the *ṣadā* or *hāmā*, a bird representing the soul of the deceased.

The phrases "صروف الليالي" (vicissitudes of nights/time) and "الجدود العواثر" (stumbling fates/fortunes) refer to the pre-Islamic cosmological concept of time and fate as powerful, often malevolent, forces.
